title: Relocating an Art Gallery or Framing Shop in Addis: Temperature and Shock Protection for Masterpieces
excerpt: Art gallery moves Addis: framing shop relocation, temperature protection, shock crating, climate control, inventory, masterpieces, commercial fine art logistics.
content:
Relocating an art gallery or framing shop in Addis: temperature and shock protection for masterpieces

Galleries and framing shops move inventory that cannot be reordered from a shelf—canvases, works on paper, glassed pieces, and client consignments awaiting exhibition. Temperature swings in Addis sun and shock from cobblestone approaches crack more than glass—they crack trust with artists and collectors.

This guide covers commercial relocation for galleries and framing businesses—climate, shock, and chain-of-custody discipline.

Inventory and condition reporting

  • Photograph each piece front and back; note existing flaws.
  • Separate consignment from owned stock—documentation per artist agreement.
  • Serial or inventory numbers on crate labels matching spreadsheet.
  • Declared value aligned with insurance schedule before wheels turn.

Temperature and climate

Avoid noon truck beds for sensitive media—load during cooler windows. Short transport duration; climate-controlled vehicle when budget allows. No sealed plastic against humid canvas long-term—breathable wrap with corner protectors.

Shock protection and crating

Framed works

Glass tape X-pattern; corner protectors; vertical travel in art bins.

Large canvases

Custom crates with foam rails; never lean unstretched works against sharp edges.

Framing shop equipment

Glass sheets vertical with separators; mat cutters secured; chopper blades boxed labeled.

Chain-of-custody

Sign-out at origin; sign-in at destination; sealed crates for high-value consignments. One move captain reconciles hourly—mislabeled crate is reputational disaster.

Gallery downtime and rehang plan

Pre-mark new wall layout; install lighting before public reopen. Soft opening for collectors by appointment before public rush.

Bole and Kazanchis adjacency moves

Diplomatic and institutional belts need manifest discipline—plates and crew IDs pre-registered. Lobby protection on marble—wheeled crates need soft wheels.

Insurance

Fine art rider explicit; owner-packed exclusions understood—vendor crating recommended for consignment.

Active Movers fine art coordination

Disclose gallery move at booking; request crating-capable crew references and climate-aware load plan.

Conclusion

Gallery and framing relocations succeed with condition reports, climate-aware timing, vertical crated travel, and custody logs—masterpieces arrive exhibition-ready, not restoration-ready.
author: Active Movers Team
